title: gilia brecciarum subsp. neglecta
intro: Gilia brecciarum subsp. neglecta is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is also known as the Desert Gilia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Gilia brecciarum subsp. neglecta include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and promoting overall well-being.
